access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q21.explanation: Câu 21 mâu thuẫn với thông tin ở đoạn A. Bài nêu: "Whale sharks are the largest fish in the world, with the longest ever recorded at over 13.5 metres. Fortunately, they are gentle filter feeders that suck in plankton and small fish. Curiously, there are rows of tiny teeth within the whale shark’s impressively large mouth, the purpose of which nobody as yet understands.". Câu hỏi đã đổi một chi tiết quan trọng hoặc đảo ngược ý trong bài, nên đáp án là FALSE.
q21.evidence: Whale sharks are the largest fish in the world, with the longest ever recorded at over 13.5 metres. Fortunately, they are gentle filter feeders that suck in plankton and small fish. Curiously, there are rows of tiny teeth within the whale shark’s impressively large mouth, the purpose of which nobody as yet understands._

q22.explanation: Câu 22 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn B, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "Maritime construction and development again cause damage and environment loss, but also lead to an increase in pollution. Pollution from land, increased boat traffic and tourism can affect whale sharks both directly and indirectly, most significantly by destroying prey populations. Another issue is that habitat destruction, elevated noise and pollution from boat traffic may affect migration patterns, and migration is an essential part of the whale shark’s life history in order to feed, mate and give birth.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q22.evidence: Maritime construction and development again cause damage and environment loss, but also lead to an increase in pollution. Pollution from land, increased boat traffic and tourism can affect whale sharks both directly and indirectly, most significantly by destroying prey populations. Another issue is that habitat destruction, elevated noise and pollution from boat traffic may affect migration patterns, and migration is an essential part of the whale shark’s life history in order to feed, mate and give birth._

q23.explanation: Câu 23 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn A. Ý chính trong bài là: "Whale sharks are the largest fish in the world, with the longest ever recorded at over 13.5 metres. Fortunately, they are gentle filter feeders that suck in plankton and small fish. Curiously, there are rows of tiny teeth within the whale shark’s impressively large mouth, the purpose of which nobody as yet understands.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q23.evidence: Whale sharks are the largest fish in the world, with the longest ever recorded at over 13.5 metres. Fortunately, they are gentle filter feeders that suck in plankton and small fish. Curiously, there are rows of tiny teeth within the whale shark’s impressively large mouth, the purpose of which nobody as yet understands._

q24.explanation: Câu 24 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn B. Ý chính trong bài là: "Healthy ecosystems are essential for the continuing presence of whale sharks and the destruction or spoiling of their environments directly affects whale shark populations. Environment loss occurs in many ways. For example, overfishing of reef fish allows population explosions of coral grazers, causing a reduction in food.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q24.evidence: Healthy ecosystems are essential for the continuing presence of whale sharks and the destruction or spoiling of their environments directly affects whale shark populations. Environment loss occurs in many ways. For example, overfishing of reef fish allows population explosions of coral grazers, causing a reduction in food._

q25.explanation: Câu 25 mâu thuẫn với thông tin ở đoạn F. Bài nêu: "The whale shark’s large size and specialised diet means this species is difficult to keep in marine zoos, and it has, as yet, not become a mainstream show species. Individuals have, however, been kept in aquaria in Japan, although scientific opinion is divided as to whether this will be able to positively affect wild populations of whale sharks.". Câu hỏi đã đổi một chi tiết quan trọng hoặc đảo ngược ý trong bài, nên đáp án là FALSE.
q25.evidence: The whale shark’s large size and specialised diet means this species is difficult to keep in marine zoos, and it has, as yet, not become a mainstream show species. Individuals have, however, been kept in aquaria in Japan, although scientific opinion is divided as to whether this will be able to positively affect wild populations of whale sharks._

q26.explanation: Câu 26 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn G, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "Whereas other plankton-feeders swim slowly through the water, mouth agape, with the forward motion driving plankton-laden water into their mouths, the whale shark distends its jaws and actively sucks, drawing water containing microscopic, free-swimming items, such as small crustaceans, fishes and squids, into its mouth. Gill rakers - special structures on the gills - filter out any organism over 2 to 3 millimetres in size, meaning that almost nothing but liquid passes back out through the gills. Whale sharks have been observed "coughing", which is thought to be a method of clearing food particles that have accumulated on the gill rakers.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q26.evidence: Whereas other plankton-feeders swim slowly through the water, mouth agape, with the forward motion driving plankton-laden water into their mouths, the whale shark distends its jaws and actively sucks, drawing water containing microscopic, free-swimming items, such as small crustaceans, fishes and squids, into its mouth. Gill rakers - special structures on the gills - filter out any organism over 2 to 3 millimetres in size, meaning that almost nothing but liquid passes back out through the gills. Whale sharks have been observed "coughing", which is thought to be a method of clearing food particles that have accumulated on the gill rakers._

q27.explanation: Câu 27 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn A. Ý chính trong bài là: "The whale shark is generally considered harmless and is not aggressive towards humans in the water. There have, however, been a few instances of boats being butted by whale sharks, possibly after the sharks had been provoked. Divers are also warned to steer clear of the powerful tail and follow the Whale Shark Code of Conduct at all times.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q27.evidence: The whale shark is generally considered harmless and is not aggressive towards humans in the water. There have, however, been a few instances of boats being butted by whale sharks, possibly after the sharks had been provoked. Divers are also warned to steer clear of the powerful tail and follow the Whale Shark Code of Conduct at all times._
